CREATE TABLE test_files (id INT, file_name VARCHAR);
INSERT INTO test_files (id, file_name) VALUES (1, tmp_file_name('A', 'B'));
INSERT INTO test_files (id, file_name) VALUES (2, tmp_file_name('TEST', ''));
INSERT INTO test_files (id, file_name) VALUES (3, tmp_file_name('', 'XYZ'));
INSERT INTO test_files (id, file_name) VALUES (4, tmp_file_name(NULL, NULL));
SELECT file_name FROM test_files WHERE id = 1;
SELECT file_name FROM test_files WHERE file_name LIKE '%B%';
INSERT INTO test_files (id, file_name) VALUES (5, tmp_file_name('PRE', 'EXT'));
SELECT tmp_file_name('PREFIX', 'EXT') AS unique_file_name FROM test_files;
SELECT tmp_file_name(NULL, 'ext') AS temp_file_name FROM (SELECT 1 AS num) AS subquery;
SELECT t1.file_name FROM test_files t1 JOIN (SELECT tmp_file_name('JOIN', 'TEST') AS join_file) t2 ON t1.id = 1;
INSERT INTO test_files (id, file_name) VALUES (6, tmp_file_name('FOO', 'EXT'));
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_files WHERE file_name IS NOT NULL;
SELECT DISTINCT file_name FROM test_files WHERE id > 0;
SELECT file_name FROM test_files WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_files WHERE id < 4);
INSERT INTO test_files (id, file_name) VALUES (7, tmp_file_name('ANOTHER', 'EXT'));
SELECT file_name, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_files) AS total_files FROM test_files;
